inputData.question: Show the breakdown of course types for each department

response.bodyRaw:
{"status":"success","steps":{"input":{"question":"Show the breakdown of course types for each department"},"sql":{"payload":{"question":"Show the breakdown of course types for each department"},"startedAt":1771804251963,"status":"success","output":{"reasoning":"Produce a per-department breakdown of course_type counts. Use distinct course_type values from courses, cross-join with all departments to ensure every department/type pair appears, then left-join aggregated counts. Order deterministically by department_name and course_type.","sql":"WITH types AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT course_type AS course_type
  FROM public.courses
), dept_types AS (
  SELECT
    d.id AS department_id,
    d.code AS department_code,
    d.name AS department_name,
    t.course_type AS course_type
  FROM public.departments d
  CROSS JOIN types t
), course_counts AS (
  SELECT
    department_id,
    course_type,
    COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM public.courses
  GROUP BY department_id, course_type
)
SELECT
  dt.department_id AS department_id,
  dt.department_code AS department_code,
  dt.department_name AS department_name,
  dt.course_type AS course_type,
  COALESCE(cc.cnt, 0) AS course_count
FROM dept_types dt
LEFT JOIN course_counts cc
  ON cc.department_id = dt.department_id
  AND cc.course_type = dt.course_type
ORDER BY dt.department_name, dt.course_type;","assumptions":["course_type values to report are the distinct values present in public.courses","include every department combined with each existing course_type (show 0 when no courses of that type exist for the department)","departments.id and courses.department_id are the correct join keys (confirmed in schema)"]},"endedAt":1771804280052},"run-raw-sql":{"payload":{"sql":"WITH types AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT course_type AS course_type
  FROM public.courses
), dept_types AS (
  SELECT
    d.id AS department_id,
    d.code AS department_code,
    d.name AS department_name,
    t.course_type AS course_type
  FROM public.departments d
  CROSS JOIN types t
), course_counts AS (
  SELECT
    department_id,
    course_type,
    COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM public.courses
  GROUP BY department_id, course_type
)
SELECT
  dt.department_id AS department_id,
  dt.department_code AS department_code,
  dt.department_name AS department_name,
  dt.course_type AS course_type,
  COALESCE(cc.cnt, 0) AS course_count
FROM dept_types dt
LEFT JOIN course_counts cc
  ON cc.department_id = dt.department_id
  AND cc.course_type = dt.course_type
ORDER BY dt.department_name, dt.course_type;"},"startedAt":1771804280057,"status":"success","output":{"ok":true,"sql":"WITH types AS (

    department_id,

    COUNT(*) AS cnt

data.department_name: Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach
Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach

Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach
Wydział Informatyki i Nauki o Materiałach

visualization.description: Stacked bar chart showing counts of each course type within each department.
visualization.title: Course type breakdown by department

resultRaw.sql:
WITH types AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT course_type AS course_type
  FROM public.courses
), dept_types AS (
  SELECT
    d.id AS department_id,
    d.code AS department_code,
    d.name AS department_name,
    t.course_type AS course_type
  FROM public.departments d
  CROSS JOIN types t
), course_counts AS (
  SELECT
    department_id,
    course_type,
    COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM public.courses
  GROUP BY department_id, course_type
)
SELECT
  dt.department_id AS department_id,
  dt.department_code AS department_code,
  dt.department_name AS department_name,
  dt.course_type AS course_type,
  COALESCE(cc.cnt, 0) AS course_count
FROM dept_types dt
LEFT JOIN course_counts cc
  ON cc.department_id = dt.department_id
  AND cc.course_type = dt.course_type
ORDER BY dt.department_name, dt.course_type;

visualization.description: Stacked bar chart showing counts of each course type within each department.
visualization.title: Course type breakdown by department

result.sql:
WITH types AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT course_type AS course_type
  FROM public.courses
), dept_types AS (
  SELECT
    d.id AS department_id,
    d.code AS department_code,
    d.name AS department_name,
    t.course_type AS course_type
  FROM public.departments d
  CROSS JOIN types t
), course_counts AS (
  SELECT
    department_id,
    course_type,
    COUNT(*) AS cnt
  FROM public.courses
  GROUP BY department_id, course_type
)
SELECT
  dt.department_id AS department_id,
  dt.department_code AS department_code,
  dt.department_name AS department_name,
  dt.course_type AS course_type,
  COALESCE(cc.cnt, 0) AS course_count
FROM dept_types dt
LEFT JOIN course_counts cc
  ON cc.department_id = dt.department_id
  AND cc.course_type = dt.course_type
ORDER BY dt.department_name, dt.course_type;
